name: notion-search
description:
Search for pages, databases, and connected sources in the user's Notion workspace.
Performs semantic search over Notion content and connected sources (Slack, Google Drive, GitHub, Jira, Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, Linear). Connected source search requires a Notion AI plan; without it, search is limited to the Notion workspace only.

## When to use this tool

- ALWAYS call this BEFORE notion-fetch when the user references a Notion page by name, title, or topic. Do NOT guess or fabricate Notion page IDs.
- ALWAYS call this when notion-fetch returns a validation_error -- the failed id was likely a page title or slug, not a valid UUID.
- Use the `id` field from search results to call notion-fetch.
- Use one search call per question for best results.

## Parameters

- `query` (required): Semantic search query -- must be at least 1 character. Use natural language describing what you are looking for. NEVER pass an empty string `""`.
- `filters` (required): Pass `{}` for no filters, or use:
   - `created_date_range`: `{"start_date": "2024-01-01", "end_date": "2025-01-01"}`
   - `created_by_user_ids`: `["user-uuid-1", "user-uuid-2"]`
- `page_size`: Max results (default 10, max 25). Do NOT exceed 25.
- `max_highlight_length`: Max chars for result highlights (default 200, 0 to omit).
- `page_url`: Restrict search to within a specific page and its sub-pages.
- `teamspace_id`: Restrict search to a specific teamspace.
- `data_source_url`: Search within a specific database data source (use `collection://...` URL from notion-fetch output, NOT the database URL).

## Workflow: search then fetch

1. Call notion-search with the user's query and `"filters": {}`.
2. From the results, pick the matching page and take its `id` field.
3. Call notion-fetch with that `id` -- it will be a valid UUID.

<example description="Basic search">{"query": "weekly standup notes", "filters": {}}</example>
<example description="Date-filtered search">{"query": "quarterly revenue", "filters": {"created_date_range": {"start_date": "2024-01-01", "end_date": "2025-01-01"}}}</example>
<example description="Search within a database">{"query": "design review", "data_source_url": "collection://f336d0bc-b841-465b-8045-024475c079dd", "filters": {}}</example>

## Presenting results to the user

- Show the page title and a brief highlight for each result.
- NEVER fabricate page titles, content, or URLs -- only use what the tool returns.
- If no results match, say so and suggest the user try different keywords.

query.description:
Semantic search query over your entire Notion workspace and connected sources (Slack, Google Drive, Github, Jira, Microsoft Teams, Sharepoint, OneDrive, or Linear). For best results, don't provide more than one question per tool call. Use a separate "search" tool call for each search you want to perform.

Alternatively, the query can be a substring or keyword to find users by matching against their name or email address. For example: "john" or "john@example.com"

data_source_url.description: Optionally, provide the URL of a Data source to search. This will perform a semantic search over the pages in the Data Source. Note: must be a Data Source, not a Database. <data-source> tags are part of the Notion flavored Markdown format returned by tools like fetch. The full spec is available in the create-pages tool description.

page_url.description: Optionally, provide the URL or ID of a page to search within. This will perform a semantic search over the content within and under the specified page. Accepts either a full page URL (e.g. https://notion.so/workspace/Page-Title-1234567890) or just the page ID (UUIDv4) with or without dashes.

teamspace_id.description: Optionally, provide the ID of a teamspace to restrict search results to. This will perform a search over content within the specified teamspace only. Accepts the teamspace ID (UUIDv4) with or without dashes.

filters.description: Optionally provide filters to apply to the search results. Only valid when query_type is 'internal'.

created_date_range.description: Optional filter to only produce search results created within the specified date range.

start_date.description: The start date of the date range as an ISO 8601 date string, if any.

end_date.description: The end date of the date range as an ISO 8601 date string, if any.

created_by_user_ids.description: Optional filter to only produce search results created by the Notion users that have the specified user IDs.

page_size.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 10). Lower values reduce response size.

max_highlight_length.description: Maximum character length for result highlights (default 200). Set to 0 to omit highlights entirely.
